machinery_genes.role: outer-membrane secretin ring (outer ring of basal body)
machinery_genes.quote: EscC was a major protein component of the outer ring in the core basal body... These results indicate that EscC, EscD, and EscJ are required for the formation of the TTS apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: Mizusaki/Abe et al 2006 (EPEC E2348/69). EscC = SctC secretin (InvG/YscC/HrcC homolog). Same gene present in O157 Sakai LEE (Perna 1998 PMID 9673266: the 41 shared LEE genes are >95% identical between O157:H7 EDL933 and EPEC E2348/69; Sakai LEE is the EHEC O157 LEE).

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane ring lipoprotein (inner ring of basal body)
machinery_genes.quote: EscF was also associated with EscJ, a presumed inner ring protein... These results indicate that EscC, EscD, and EscJ are required for the formation of the TTS apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: Mizusaki/Abe et al 2006 (EPEC). EscJ = SctJ inner-ring lipoprotein (PrgK/YscJ/EscJ). Transferred to O157 Sakai via the shared LEE (Perna 1998).

machinery_genes.role: inner-membrane MS-ring component (basal body inner membrane)
machinery_genes.quote: The EscC outer ring protein was associated with both the EscF needle protein and EscD, a presumed inner membrane protein.
machinery_genes.note: Mizusaki/Abe et al 2006 (EPEC). EscD = SctD (PrgH/YscD/EscD family, single-span TM with periplasmic domain). Also named in Pathogens 2025 (PMID 41305337): 'escD forms part of the inner membrane of the basal body'. Same LEE gene in O157 Sakai.

machinery_genes.quote: The export apparatus is assembled from five highly conserved membrane proteins: escR, escS, escT, escU, and escV.
machinery_genes.note: Pathogens 2025 in-silico EPEC LEE T3SS analysis; proteins benchmarked at >96% identity against O157:H7 EDL933 reference. EscR = SctR (SpaP/YscR). Same gene in O157 Sakai LEE.

machinery_genes.quote: The export apparatus is assembled from five highly conserved membrane proteins: escR, escS, escT, escU, and escV.
machinery_genes.note: Pathogens 2025 (EPEC LEE; EDL933-referenced). EscS = SctS (SpaQ/YscS). Same gene in O157 Sakai LEE.

machinery_genes.quote: The export apparatus is assembled from five highly conserved membrane proteins: escR, escS, escT, escU, and escV.
machinery_genes.note: Pathogens 2025 (EPEC LEE; EDL933-referenced). EscT = SctT (SpaR/YscT). Same gene in O157 Sakai LEE.

machinery_genes.quote: Orf16 interacts with the C-terminal domain of an inner membrane component of the secretion apparatus, EscU; the inner rod protein EscI; the needle protein EscF
machinery_genes.note: Monjaras Feria et al 2012 (EPEC E2348/69). EscU = SctU (SpaS/YscU/EscU) export-gate autoprotease that, with EscP, drives the early-to-translocator substrate switch. Also named in the export-apparatus quintet by Pathogens 2025. Same gene in O157 Sakai LEE.

machinery_genes.quote: As one of the largest export apparatus proteins, escV, is responsible for controlling the export gate of the T3SS
machinery_genes.note: Pathogens 2025 (EPEC LEE; shares 97.9% identity with O157:H7 EDL933 reference). EscV = SctV (InvA/YscV/EscV/MxiA/HrcV). Same gene in O157 Sakai LEE.

machinery_genes.quote: escN, escL, and escO comprise the cytosolic ATPase complex... escN hydrolyzes ATP to provide the energy needed for effector secretion
machinery_genes.note: Pathogens 2025 (EPEC LEE; EDL933-referenced). EscN = SctN (InvC/YscN/EscN) ATPase. Same gene in O157 Sakai LEE.

machinery_genes.role: ATPase stalk / central stalk (positive regulator of EscN)
machinery_genes.quote: EscO is essential for secretion of all categories of T3SS substrates... it was found to interact with the ATPase EscN and its negative regulator, EscL... EscO is the virulence counterpart of the flagellar FliJ protein.
machinery_genes.note: Romo-Castillo et al 2014 (EPEC). EscO (formerly Orf15/EscA) = SctO ATPase stalk (YscO/InvI/FliJ analog). Same gene in O157 Sakai LEE.

machinery_genes.role: ATPase stator / negative regulator (C-ring to ATPase link)
machinery_genes.quote: it was found to interact with the ATPase EscN and its negative regulator, EscL, of the export apparatus.
machinery_genes.note: Romo-Castillo et al 2014 (EPEC). EscL = SctL (OrgB/YscL/MxiN) ATPase-complex stator/regulator; also named in the cytosolic ATPase complex (escN/escL/escO) by Pathogens 2025. Same gene in O157 Sakai LEE.

machinery_genes.role: inner rod (periplasmic rod connecting basal body rings to needle)
machinery_genes.quote: Orf16 is a T3SS-secreted substrate and regulates the secretion of the inner rod component EscI.
machinery_genes.note: Monjaras Feria et al 2012 (EPEC E2348/69). EscI = SctI inner rod (PrgJ/YscI/EscI). Same gene in O157 Sakai LEE.

machinery_genes.quote: The EscC outer ring protein was associated with both the EscF needle protein and EscD
machinery_genes.note: Mizusaki/Abe et al 2006 (EPEC). EscF = SctF needle subunit (PrgI/YscF/MxiH). Same gene in O157 Sakai LEE.

machinery_genes.quote: Orf16 acts as a molecular measuring device in a way similar to that of members of the Yersinia YscP and flagellar FliK protein family. Therefore, we propose that this protein be renamed EscP.
machinery_genes.note: Monjaras Feria et al 2012 (EPEC E2348/69). EscP (Orf16) = SctP needle-length ruler (InvJ/YscP/Spa32). Same gene in O157 Sakai LEE.

machinery_genes.role: needle-tip filament sheath / translocation conduit to host cell
machinery_genes.quote: All of the genes necessary for this process are present in the locus of enterocyte effacement, which encodes a type III secretion system, the secreted Esp proteins and the surface protein intimin... a deletion of the espA gene completely abolished the capacity of such STEC mutants to bind to HeLa cells and to induce actin rearrangements.
machinery_genes.note: Ebel et al 1998 study explicitly on Shiga-toxin-producing E. coli O157 (STEC/EHEC, the same pathotype as Sakai): EspA forms filamentous surface appendages atop the EscF needle that deliver effectors. Translocon-associated apparatus component, not an effector. Same LEE gene in O157 Sakai (LEE4 cluster).

machinery_genes.quote: the effector protein espA forms a filament, and espB and espD create the translocation pore.
machinery_genes.note: Pathogens 2025 (EPEC LEE; EDL933-referenced). EspD = hydrophobic translocator forming the host-membrane pore (apparatus per the brief, not an effector). LEE4 gene; same gene in O157 Sakai LEE.

machinery_genes.quote: the effector protein espA forms a filament, and espB and espD create the translocation pore.
machinery_genes.note: Pathogens 2025 (EPEC LEE; EDL933-referenced). EspB = translocator forming the host-membrane pore with EspD (apparatus per the brief, despite being secreted). LEE4 gene; same gene in O157 Sakai LEE.

machinery_genes.quote: These proteins, known as gatekeepers, prevent premature effector secretion before host cell contact is established... SepL belongs to a family of proteins whose members include MxiC from [Shigella] ... InvE and SsaL from [Salmonella] pathogenicity islands 1 and 2.
machinery_genes.note: Deng/Wang/Gonzalez-Pedrajo work, here Portaliou et al 2018 (EPEC E2348/69). SepL = SctW gatekeeper (MxiC/InvE/YopN homolog), apparatus-associated cytoplasmic component controlling the secretion hierarchy. LEE-encoded; same gene in O157 Sakai LEE. SepD/CesL (its partners) excluded: SepD is a co-regulator and CesL is a chaperone.

notes: System: the single functional injectisome in E. coli O157:H7 Sakai is the LEE-encoded (locus of enterocyte effacement) T3SS, using the Esc/Sep/Esp nomenclature established by Elliott et al 1998 (EPEC E2348/69). The instance label 'prophage_Sp9' and the curated effectors (ECs1567/1568/1812/1814/1815/1821/1824/1825) are NON-LEE effectors scattered in lambdoid prophages; Tobe et al 2006 (PNAS, the Sakai effector-repertoire paper) shows these prophage-borne effectors are all translocated by this one LEE-encoded T3SS ('the majority of functional effector genes are encoded by nine exchangeable effector loci that lie within lambdoid prophages'). The apparatus genes therefore are the LEE esc/sep/esp structural genes, NOT genes on Sp9 itself. STRAIN TRANSFER: most naming quotes are from the model EPEC strain E2348/69 (Mizusaki 2006, Monjaras Feria 2012, Romo-Castillo 2014, Portaliou 2018) or generic EPEC in-silico analysis (Pathogens 2025). This is justified because Perna et al 1998 sequenced the O157:H7 EDL933 LEE and showed the 41 LEE genes shared with E2348/69 are >95% identical, and EDL933 LEE == the EHEC O157 LEE that Sakai also carries; the Pathogens 2025 paper benchmarks the EPEC Esc proteins directly against O157:H7 EDL933 at 96.8-97.9% identity. The Ebel 1998 EspA quote is on STEC/EHEC O157 directly. Core apparatus fully covered gene-by-gene: secretin EscC, inner ring EscJ, MS-ring EscD, export-gate quintet EscR/S/T/U/V, ATPase complex EscN/EscO/EscL, inner rod EscI, needle EscF, needle-length ruler EscP, translocon EspA/EspB/EspD, gatekeeper SepL (18 machinery genes). DELIBERATELY EXCLUDED as non-machinery: all secreted effectors including the Sp9 ECs effectors and LEE effectors Tir/Map/EspF/EspG/EspH/EspZ; chaperones CesT/CesD/CesL/CesAB; the SctK/SortingPlatform genes EscK(Orf4) and EscQ/SepQ were NOT individually listed because no verbatim in-EPEC naming quote naming them as apparatus was secured in this pass (they belong to the same LEE operons and are genuine C-ring components; their omission is conservative, the only gap in an otherwise complete core); LEE regulators Ler/GrlA/GrlR; intimin Eae and receptor Tir (adhesin/effector, not injectisome). LOW-CONFIDENCE: EspB and EspD are listed as translocon apparatus per the brief's explicit instruction (translocon proteins are apparatus, not effectors) although they are secreted. All quotes verbatim from the cited PMC full texts or PubMed abstracts; all PMIDs/DOIs verified via get_article_metadata.
